name: Greater Des Moines Habitat For Humanity
description_short: Greater Des Moines Habitat for Humanity builds affordable housing and provides community support services to low-income families in central Iowa.
description_long:
Greater Des Moines Habitat for Humanity is a nonprofit organization affiliated with the international Habitat for Humanity movement, operating in the Des Moines metropolitan area. The organization's primary mission focuses on combating poverty housing by building and renovating affordable homes for families in need. Since its establishment as a local affiliate, it has worked to address housing insecurity in central Iowa through community-based development and partnership models.

The organization provides multiple services beyond homebuilding, including affordable housing solutions, community programs, and support services for low-income families. Their work typically involves constructing new homes, rehabilitating existing properties, and providing homeownership education to residents. They engage volunteers from the community to participate in building projects and support their mission of creating stable housing opportunities.

Greater Des Moines Habitat for Humanity distinguishes itself through its volunteer-driven model and nonprofit status, ensuring all proceeds support their housing mission rather than shareholder returns. As a faith-based organization, they integrate values-driven community service into their operations. Their local focus on the Des Moines area allows for targeted impact and deep community relationships, though this limits their geographic reach compared to larger national nonprofits.

While their mission is genuine and impactful, prospective applicants should understand that Habitat homes typically require significant sweat equity (volunteer hours) from future homeowners and involve strict qualification criteria. The organization's website access restrictions may limit transparency for researchers, and their capacity to serve is limited by funding and volunteer availability. For families seeking immediate housing assistance or financial counseling, they may not be the sole solution but serve as a valuable long-term housing development resource.
pros: Nonprofit organization with no profit motive — all resources directed to mission
Volunteer-based model reduces costs and builds community engagement
Provides actual affordable homeownership, not just temporary assistance
Part of established international network with proven track record
Faith-based values emphasize dignity and long-term family stability
Local Des Moines focus enables deep community knowledge and relationships
Addresses root cause (housing) rather than symptoms of poverty
cons: Website access restrictions limit transparency and information availability for potential applicants
Requires significant sweat equity (volunteer hours) from homeowners, creating barriers for working families
Limited geographic service area restricted to Des Moines metropolitan region
Capacity constraints mean long waiting lists and inability to serve all qualified applicants
Does not provide emergency financial assistance or credit counseling services
best_for: Low-income families seeking long-term affordable homeownership in the Des Moines area
Community members wanting to volunteer and contribute to housing solutions
Families willing to invest sweat equity in exchange for reduced-cost homeownership
diagnosis: Greater Des Moines Habitat for Humanity is best for low-income families committed to homeownership and able to contribute volunteer labor, not for those needing immediate financial assistance or credit repair. The main caveat is that their capacity is limited, requirements are strict, and their services focus exclusively on housing rather than broader financial wellness or credit counseling.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 95 University Ave, Suite 1, Des Moines, IA 50314
